title: Tightly packing storage slots using left shifts and bitwise OR
yul_optimized:
let packed := or(shl(96, receiver), feeNumerator)
sstore(slot, packed)
explanation: Consolidates multiple independent state updates into a single atomic storage write operation (`sstore`). By shifting a clean 160-bit address left by 96 bits, the lower 96 bits are completely zeroed out. Performing a bitwise `or` with an unshifted integer bound to 96 bits seamlessly nests both properties into a single 32-byte layout with zero extraction overlap or padding overhead.
when_to_apply: Whenever an address configuration parameters set fits structurally alongside variable properties inside a single 32-byte threshold limit.
when_not_to_apply: If the trailing parameter range exceeds the remaining byte room available inside the targeted storage slot configuration.
